title: Some, Too Fragile For Winter Winds,
content:
Some, too fragile for winter winds,
    The thoughtful grave encloses, --
    Tenderly tucking them in from frost
    Before their feet are cold.

    Never the treasures in her nest
    The cautious grave exposes,
    Building where schoolboy dare not look
    And sportsman is not bold.

    This covert have all the children
    Early aged, and often cold, --
    Sparrows unnoticed by the Father;
    Lambs for whom time had not a fold.
meaning:
This evocative piece by Emily Elizabeth Dickinson, titled "Some, Too Fragile For Winter Winds,", represents a masterful exploration of classic. The lines capture a profound emotional resonance...

### Why We Love This Line
At Linespedia, we believe that poetry is the ultimate sanctuary for the soul...
